label: verify-frazer-v-dixon-unified-school-dist
system:
You are the citation verifier in a legal-exploration pipeline. You are given one authority as cited by the pipeline's components, plus snippets showing how it was used. Using web search (prefer primary/official sources: leginfo.legislature.ca.gov, courts.ca.gov, law.justia.com, casetext.com, codes.findlaw.com, fppc.ca.gov), determine:
1. Does the authority exist?
2. Is the citation form correct (reporter cite, year, section number)?
3. Does it plausibly stand for what the snippets use it for?

Statuses: VERIFIED (exists, cite essentially right, usage consistent with what you found); MISCHARACTERIZED (exists but does not support the claimed use, or the cite attaches to the wrong case/section); NOT_FOUND (cannot locate after genuine searching); CONTESTED (real sources point in conflicting directions, or scope is genuinely unsettled); UNCLEAR (could not determine within a reasonable search).

You are NOT determining whether the authority is still good law (no Shepardizing) — say what you actually confirmed, no more. Be strict: a close-but-wrong reporter cite or a holding stretched past what sources show is MISCHARACTERIZED, not VERIFIED.
prompt:
## Authority as cited (variants seen in the documents)

- Frazer v. Dixon Unified School Dist

## How the documents used it

[delphi] … characterized differently, see Disagreements)
- Gov. Code § 54952.2(c) — technological devices/electronic communications (2, one with a differing description)
- *Roberts v. City of Palmdale* (1993) 5 Cal.4th 363 (2)
- *Frazer v. Dixon Unified School Dist.* (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 781 (2)

**Cited by only one panelist:**
- Cal. Const. art. I, § 3(b) (Prop 59)
- Gov. Code §§ 54960 and 54960.5 — standing and fee-shifting
- *Sacramento Newspaper Guild v. Sacramento County Bd.…

[dialectic] …h council member and, in each subsequent briefing, said "Member X was persuaded by the density-bonus argument," any Brown Act practitioner in California would say the safe harbor was lost and a serial meeting occurred. *Frazer v. Dixon Unified School Dist.* (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 781 supports the broader proposition that a chain of individual, one-on-one conversations among board members about a specific pending personnel/policy matter — with no full-body gathering — can …

[naive_sonnet] …deliberate, or take action"?

Run the elements:

- **Majority**: 4 of 5 members — satisfied on its face. The doctrine (built around "hub and spoke" and "daisy chain" fact patterns, e.g., the line of authority following *Frazer v. Dixon Unified School Dist.* (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 781 — verify pin cite) was developed *precisely* to reach situations where no two members were ever in a room together, but a common conduit passed positions between them serially, over time. The…

## Context

The underlying question is a California public-agency hypothetical: q1 — The AI assistant as serial-meeting intermediary (Brown Act).

Verify this authority per your instructions.

canonical_cite.description: the correct full citation, if determinable

note.description: 1-3 sentences: what was confirmed or refuted, and the correct cite

text: {"status":"MISCHARACTERIZED","canonical_cite":"Frazer v. Dixon Unified School Dist. (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 781","note":"The case and reporter cite are real and accurate (3d DCA, 1993), but it is a textbook-selection/curriculum-review-committee due process dispute — it does not address the Brown Act, serial meetings, or the \"hub and spoke\"/\"daisy chain\" doctrine the documents cite it for. The pipeline appears to have attached a real citation to an invented holding.","source_urls":["https://law.justia.com/cases/california/court-of-appeal/4th/18/781.html"]}

data.canonical_cite: Frazer v. Dixon Unified School Dist. (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 781
data.note: The case and reporter cite are real and accurate (3d DCA, 1993), but it is a textbook-selection/curriculum-review-committee due process dispute — it does not address the Brown Act, serial meetings, or the "hub and spoke"/"daisy chain" doctrine the documents cite it for. The pipeline appears to have attached a real citation to an invented holding.
